scope.review_target: LFG step-3 implementation work for plan docs/plans/2026-04-25-005-feat-kh-export-surface-plan.md

intent: Add an additive kh-compatible export surface inside pirata workspace so the second kh ingest attempt does not lose manifest content to the .jsonl suffix filter. Three deliverables: idempotent builder script, hermetic smoke test, refreshed paste-now Codex prompt.

reviewers_skipped.security: no auth, no user input, no public endpoints, no permissions
reviewers_skipped.performance: no DB queries, no async, simple file I/O on small files (1 movie, 300 manifest rows)
reviewers_skipped.api-contract: no API surface; CLI args are local-only and stable
reviewers_skipped.adversarial: diff <50 lines of executable code path; no auth/payments/data-mutation surface that warrants stress-test persona
reviewers_skipped.cli-readiness: CLI is internal, 2 args, low-risk; stack-specific persona not warranted

advisory_findings.title: Source per-movie JSON read uses default encoding
advisory_findings.why_it_matters: build_movie_md calls json_path.read_text() without encoding='utf-8'. Python 3 defaults to locale-driven encoding, which is utf-8 on macOS but cp1252 on Windows. The project is a personal Mac workspace ('Personal Mac-based media workspace' per CLAUDE.md), so this is a non-issue today. Flagged for future portability if pirata ever runs on a non-macOS host.
advisory_findings.suggested_fix: Add encoding='utf-8' to json_path.read_text() in build_movie_md for symmetry with build_manifest_json which already uses encoding='utf-8'.

advisory_findings.title: No test for missing-kb error path (exit 1)
advisory_findings.why_it_matters: The build script returns exit 1 when kb/ does not exist. The smoke test never exercises this branch. Low-impact because the live pirata workspace always has kb/, but coverage gap is real.
advisory_findings.suggested_fix: Add a 23rd assertion that runs python3 scripts/build_kh_export.py --kb /tmp/nonexistent-kb-XXXX and asserts exit code is 1.

advisory_findings.title: Test tree checksum uses xargs without -0 (filename edge cases)
advisory_findings.why_it_matters: find ... | sort | xargs shasum breaks on filenames with spaces, newlines, or special chars. Pirata's kebab-case slug convention guarantees no spaces today, so this is theoretical for this project. Documented anti-pattern but project-correct.
advisory_findings.suggested_fix: Use find ... -print0 | sort -z | xargs -0 shasum if multi-platform safety is wanted. Skip otherwise.

R1.evidence: test assertion 04 + 08 (per-movie JSON exported, byte-identical to source)

R2.evidence: test assertion 05 + 10a-10d (MD wrapper exists, contains all 4 literal strings)

R3.evidence: test assertion 11 (manifest.json schema verified, row_count=300 matches source)

R5.evidence: scripts/build_kh_export.py exists, test assertion 17 verifies idempotency via tree-checksum match across runs

R6.evidence: scripts/tests/test_kh_export.sh exists with 22 assertion labels (25 distinct PASS lines), all PASS

R7.evidence: docs/prompts/2026-04-25-kh-ingest-FIRE-v2.md exists, staging commands cite kb/kh-export/04-derived/ (9 occurrences), validation-result decision is GO-WITH-CAVEATS

R9.evidence: test assertion 14 (kb/manifest.jsonl SHA unchanged before/after); manual verification: no writes detected under /Users/vidigal/knowledge-base/

requirements_check.summary: 9 of 9 plan requirements have implementation evidence verified by the smoke test.

patterns_followed: scripts/contact_sheet.py:327-330 atomic write idiom (adapted to dir-level via tmp+os.replace; verified)
scripts/imdb_ingest.py exit-code convention 0/1/2/3 (verified in main() try/except)
scripts/tests/test_imdb_ingest.sh structure (PASS/FAIL counters, set -uo pipefail without -e, sectional run blocks; verified)

verdict: Ready to merge. 0 P0/P1/P2 findings. 3 P3 advisory items below the autofix threshold (per caller instruction 'Skip P2/P3 cosmetic'). 9/9 plan requirements verified by 25-assertion smoke test. All 5 cited project patterns are mirrored in the implementation.
